bull.b: A very high Autoship share creates predictable, recurring demand that reduces CAC volatility and supports stable reorder frequency. Over 2–6 months this underpins revenue visibility, inventory planning, higher customer LTV, and a steadier base for margin and FCF improvements.

Sizable and improving free cash flow provides durable internal funding for capex, tuck-in M&A, buybacks and debt reduction. FCF at ~81% of net income signals earnings quality and gives management flexibility to invest in clinics, AI and strategic initiatives over the coming quarters.

Meaningful year-over-year adjusted EBITDA expansion reflects operational leverage from higher gross margin and SG&A discipline. Structural margin progress increases resilience to cost swings, funds reinvestment in strategic growth (vet clinics, AI), and supports sustainable profit conversion as scale grows.

bear.b: Very narrow margins leave limited buffer against inflation, fuel surcharges, supplier price moves or weaker ASPs. For a volume-driven retail model, small adverse cost or mix shifts can materially erode net income and free cash flow, constraining long-term capital allocation flexibility.

bear.t: Meaningful Leverage For A Low‑Margin Retailer
bear.b: Material leverage increases sensitivity to rising rates or slower cash conversion. The new term loan adds interest expense (management cites ~$10–15M) and while FCF is strong, deleveraging will take time; leverage constrains optionality for aggressive investment if margins slip.

bear.t: Slower Customer Net Adds & Premiumization Headwinds
bear.b: A durable slowdown in new-customer acquisition and weaker premiumization/attach rates compresses future revenue growth and NSPAC upside. Over several quarters this limits compounding revenue per customer and reduces the effectiveness of scale-driven margin gains and lifetime value expansion.
